title: The Red-Headed League
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
known_caveats: First-person narration is Watson's account; Holmes's reasoning is reconstructed through dialogue
Story is fiction; no facts to verify externally - internal consistency is the only ground truth
Some character details (Ezekiah Hopkins, the League's bequest) are themselves fabrications within the story
Geographical and temporal references are Victorian London; some terms require period context
The Red-Headed League is a famous public-domain story and may be partly known to the model before source ingestion
prior_containment_risks: Importing remembered plot details that are not present in the supplied source excerpt
Resolving aliases from model memory before the source text establishes the identity link
Treating fabricated in-story claims as externally true facts
Answering from Holmes-story prior knowledge instead of compiled source evidence
